title: Agentic Living Doc Harness
subtitle: Run, diagnose, repair, prove, and resume living-doc work with less user intervention.
scope: Living Doc Compositor headless Codex harness, stop-negotiation layer, skill routing, proof/evidence bundles, blocker semantics, inference traces, and local dashboard.
sourceCoverage: Created from the May 7, 2026 session on agentic living-doc loops, chemistry repair skills, TrackAndBack production-hardening proof/dashboard patterns, the objective-acceptance-shaping skill output, GitHub epic #185 with implementation tickets #186-#193, lifecycle proof issues #194-#199, and reviewer-inference verdict issue #200. Added relationship-map convergence type and relationship map section on May 8, 2026 to make Mermaid-backed structural projections a governed semantic surface. Added relationship-map-alignment repair skill on May 8, 2026 so diagram projections can be checked against objective and acceptance criteria. Added repair-skill runner contract implementation on May 8, 2026: shared inference-unit runner, ordered balance-scan plus repair-skill chain, lifecycle integration, dashboard bundle visibility, and contract tests. Added inference-unit graph dashboard integration tickets #209-#213 on May 9, 2026 to turn the static dashboard mockup into a real artifact-derived lifecycle graph. Implemented the first graph backend contract slice on May 9, 2026: lifecycle list endpoint, lifecycle graph endpoint, artifact-to-graph mapper, and focused dashboard server contract proof. Repaired the dashboard graph UI on May 9, 2026 after review showed the first implementation was too far from the mockup: the dashboard now replaces the old dashboard surface with a standalone graph-first application shell, lifecycle selector rail, primary lifecycle graph workspace, compact draggable inference-unit cards, fixed-size 2400 by 1400 grid canvas independent of graph contents, resettable v2 layout persistence, SVG contract arrows with labels mounted on the connector body, inspector, and local evidence tail access from graph nodes. Removed the old run-control and run-list surfaces from the dashboard on May 9, 2026 because runs are lower-level graph artifacts, not a second dashboard selection model. Refined the lifecycle selector rail on May 9, 2026 so it is a wider primary lifecycle panel with compact status/time/iteration cards instead of raw run-style cards. Removed the bottom step-plan strip and restyled the side inspector on May 9, 2026 so node-card clicks and contract-arrow clicks both open the same evidence pane. Added the operated living-doc graph node and change/commit edges on May 9, 2026 so repair units that modify the doc point back to the document with changed-file and commit metadata in the arrow inspector. Enlarged the operated living-doc card and corrected leftward change-edge routing on May 9, 2026 so labels sit on the connector body and no controller-to-document line crosses the canvas. Added repeatable Playwright browser proof for the dashboard graph on May 9, 2026 so canvas layout, card clicks, arrow clicks, inspector contents, absence of old surfaces, and change-edge geometry are enforced by the contract suite. Unified reviewer inference with the shared contract-bound inference-unit primitive on May 9, 2026: reviewer verdicts still publish the legacy lifecycle verdict artifact, but execution/proof now flows through inference-units/iteration-N/02-reviewer-inference with prompt, input contract, requiredInspectionPaths, codex-events log, last message, structured result, validation, and iteration-proof references. Added worker inference-unit snapshots on May 9, 2026 so every worker run now has inference-units/iteration-N/01-worker artifacts with prompt, input contract, copied codex-events/stderr/last-message logs, structured worker output contract, validation, and graph-preferred artifact paths while preserving the legacy codex-turns surface. Added closure-review inference units on May 9, 2026 so closed transitions require inference-units/iteration-N/03-closure-review with prompt, input contract, required evidence paths, result, validation, iteration-proof references, and dashboard graph visibility before terminal closure is persisted. Fixed issue #214 on May 9, 2026 so lifecycle-result.json and lifecycle-iteration-complete events now preserve closureReviewResultPath for closed iterations; fresh standalone calibration ldhl-20260509T080500Z-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration proves the top-level lifecycle summary, event log, iteration proof, and graph all point to the same closure-review unit. Tightened the objective on May 9, 2026 after issue #215 exposed the remaining orchestration mistake: only the initial worker and reviewer are fixed bootstrap units; every later closure, repair, readiness, blocker, or next-worker unit must be selected from the previous output contract and current proof state. Implemented the #215 contract slice on May 9, 2026: iteration finalization now builds a post-review selection contract, dispatches closure or repair from that selected next unit, writes selection artifacts into proof and output-input, passes closure review the finalized evidence snapshot, and converts denied closure review into a true-blocked lifecycle result instead of aborting. Implemented the #208 repair-skill commit policy on May 9, 2026: repair-skill inference units now run under commit-intent-only policy, their input contracts forbid direct git index mutation, result contracts carry commitIntent, commit-policy failures get the distinct repair-skill-commit-policy-blocked reason code, and dashboard-started lifecycle ldhl-20260509T103538Z-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration proved a two-iteration repair-to-closure chain with catalytic-repair-run emitting commitIntent before the next worker and closure-review.  Added fresh live lifecycle proof on May 9, 2026: standalone run ldhl-20260509T111849Z-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration used real repair-skill inference units under commit-intent-only policy, dynamically followed balance-scan output to catalytic-repair-run and objective-execution-readiness, wrote repair-chain and output-input artifacts, exposed the chain in the dashboard graph, and closed on the second iteration after closure-review.
objective: Implement a governed standalone agentic living-doc harness built around contract-bound inference units: prompt + contract input + evidence paths + context -> headless inference run -> structured result + proof log. The harness must carry a living doc instance from its initial objective-bearing state toward that instance's objective completion with minimal user intervention through a system-owned lifecycle controller. The only predetermined inference sequence is the bootstrap pair: initial worker inference does objective work, then reviewer inference directly inspects the worker's raw JSONL logs and emits the authoritative structured stop signal. After that reviewer signal, continuation must be dynamic and contract-driven: closure review, balance scan, ordered repair skills, readiness checks, blocker recording, or a next worker iteration may run only when selected by the previous output contract and current living-doc/proof state. The controller starts and chains selected inference units, passes file paths, validates contracts, persists artifacts, renders living docs, and enforces transitions, but it must not replace the intelligence of the units or hard-code a fixed post-review chain. Deterministic code may validate schemas, enforce hard gates, write artifacts, and execute allowed transitions, but it must not be the primary meaning-maker for closure, continuation, repair ordering, repair judgment, readiness, proof sufficiency, or post-review unit selection. The work is complete only when one lifecycle command can dynamically chain these contract-bound inference units until a run reaches a valid terminal state inspectable from durable artifacts and native inference traces rather than chat memory, wrapper summaries, worker self-report, recommended-skill lists, static orchestration, or deterministic proxy closure.
successCondition: All acceptance criteria in this living doc are satisfied by source-system evidence: a system-owned lifecycle controller command, isolated run process outside current user-session inference, persisted run artifacts, native inference-log inspection, reviewer-inference stop verdicts based on direct raw worker JSONL inspection, a shared contract-bound inference unit primitive, deterministic enforcement of unit contracts, dynamic post-review unit selection from output contracts, lifecycle-owned balance-scan inference when repair is selected, independent ordered repair-skill inference execution when repair is selected, rendered living-doc updates between repair attempts, sanitized evidence bundles, local dashboard views, trace references, blocker records, and contract tests that prevent closure or continuation from trusting worker self-report, wrapper summaries, deterministic proxy checks, recommended repair-skill lists, or any predetermined post-review unit chain as the authoritative completion signal.

objectiveFacets.name: Contract-bound inference unit
objectiveFacets.description: The basic system primitive is prompt plus contract input plus evidence paths plus context, executed as headless inference, producing a structured result and proof log.

objectiveFacets.name: Owned lifecycle controller
objectiveFacets.description: One system-owned command loops worker, evidence, stop negotiation, repair or resume, and finalization iterations until the living doc reaches a valid terminal state.

objectiveFacets.name: Standalone headless Codex runner
objectiveFacets.description: The harness starts a bounded Codex run outside the user's active session from a living-doc JSON objective.

objectiveFacets.name: Frozen run contract
objectiveFacets.description: Each run persists contract, state, event, skill, trace, and artifact metadata outside chat history.

objectiveFacets.name: Native inference-log evidence
objectiveFacets.description: The harness inspects native headless inference logs as hard evidence instead of relying on wrapper summaries.

objectiveFacets.name: Stop negotiation

objectiveFacets.name: Reviewer inference verdict

objectiveFacets.name: Dynamic post-review continuation

objectiveFacets.name: Skill routing

objectiveFacets.name: Living-doc repair loop

objectiveFacets.name: True block mode
objectiveFacets.description: Unrepairable stops become explicit blockers with reason codes, owning layers, required decisions, and unblock criteria.

objectiveFacets.name: Proof and evidence bundles

objectiveFacets.name: No-longer-legitimate regressions
objectiveFacets.description: Mistakes that are no longer ambiguous because the objective and acceptance criteria explicitly forbid them.

objectiveFacets.name: Relationship map projections
objectiveFacets.description: Mermaid-backed relationship maps make process, proof, and repair relationships inspectable without treating diagrams as proof or decoration.

invariants.name: Prompt, contract, evidence, log required
invariants.statement: An inference actor is not a valid lifecycle unit unless it has a role prompt, contract input, evidence paths, structured result, validation outcome, and its own proof log.

invariants.name: Worker stop is raw evidence
invariants.statement: Do not trust the worker's self-reported stop reason or wrapper summary as authoritative; stop-negotiation must infer the stop from native inference logs, trace, tools, edits, proof, and doc state.

invariants.name: Standalone or invalid
invariants.statement: A harness run that depends on the active user-session inference cannot prove this objective because it can interfere with the session it is supposed to observe and repair.

invariants.name: Native logs or invalid
invariants.statement: A stop diagnosis or closure verdict is invalid unless it is grounded in native headless inference logs, not only wrapper logs or summarized output.

invariants.name: Graph derived from artifacts
invariants.statement: The inference-unit graph is invalid if nodes or edges are hard-coded from the mockup instead of derived from lifecycle-result.json, run contracts, unit prompts, input contracts, evidence paths, raw JSONL logs, result artifacts, validation artifacts, terminal states, blockers, and issue refs.

invariants.name: Post-review chain is not static
invariants.statement: After the initial worker and reviewer bootstrap, no closure, repair, readiness, blocker, or next-worker inference unit may run merely because it is prewired in the controller; it must be selected from the previous output contract and current living-doc/proof state.

invariants.name: True blockers stop the loop
invariants.statement: A true blocker must produce a blocker record and dashboard-visible reason before any further implementation attempt continues.

invariants.name: Proof before closure
invariants.statement: A closure claim is invalid unless reviewer inference has emitted a structured closure verdict and objective terms, acceptance criteria, proof state, evidence bundle, deterministic hard gates, and dashboard gate state agree.

invariants.name: Diagram is not proof
invariants.statement: A Mermaid-backed relationship map can guide inspection and expose structure, but it is invalid as proof unless anchored to living-doc sections, criteria, invariants, issues, code, or evidence artifacts that can be inspected directly.

invariants.name: Diagram relationship must anchor to criteria
invariants.statement: A relationship-map diagram may guide reasoning only when its claim and Mermaid-implied relationship are anchored to objective terms and acceptance criteria or explicitly marked out-of-scope, blocked, stale, or diagnostic-only.

sections.title: Status Snapshot
sections.rationale: Keeps the current objective state visible against the corrected acceptance boundary: reviewer inference must produce the stop signal, while deterministic code only enforces the protocol.

stats.label: Formula
stats.value: prompt + contract + evidence paths -> result + proof log

callout.title: Architecture center: contract-bound inference units
items: The basic system primitive is prompt + contract input + evidence paths + context -> headless inference run -> structured result + proof log.
Worker, reviewer, balance scan, repair skill, readiness, and closure review should all be explicit instances of this primitive.
Context may include summaries, but required evidence paths must still be inspected when the contract says so.
The lifecycle controller chains units and validates artifacts; it does not substitute deterministic reasoning for unit judgment.
Issue #207 owns the shared primitive definition. Issues #202-#206 specialize it for repair-skill execution.

sections.title: Operating Decisions
sections.rationale: Captures the choices that define the target system before tickets or implementation slices are created.

data.name: Harness must run standalone
data.decision: The harness must run Codex headless outside the user's active session.
data.rationale: If the run depends on the same interactive session that is supervising it, the harness can interfere with the inference it is meant to observe and repair.
notes: This is an objective constraint, not an implementation preference.

data.name: Native inference logs are required
data.decision: Stop diagnosis, repair routing, blocker records, and closure checks must inspect native headless inference logs.
data.rationale: Wrapper summaries can make a run look coherent while hiding the actual reasoning and tool evidence that explain why the worker stopped.
notes: Wrapper output may be useful context, but it is not proof.

data.name: Dashboard is a first-class objective surface
data.decision: The first implementation must include a strong local dashboard, not only dashboard-ready JSON.
data.rationale: The dashboard is how unresolved boundaries, blockers, traces, and proof gates become visible enough for reduced user intervention.
notes: This tightens the earlier objective that allowed a dashboard-ready data surface.

data.name: Stop diagnosis is separate from the worker
data.decision: The worker should run naturally; a separate stop-negotiation inference layer diagnoses why it stopped.
data.rationale: Prompting the worker to produce clean stop reasons may create premature stop behavior.
notes: The worker stop is evidence, not a trusted classification.

data.name: Use TrackAndBack proof harness as pattern
data.decision: Borrow the durable contract, state, event, evidence bundle, governance verdict, dashboard gate, and native trace ideas from TrackAndBack without copying its parser-specific proof domain.
data.rationale: TrackAndBack proves the value of dashboard-visible proof gates and true blocker records around headless iterative inference.
notes: /Users/rene/projects/trackandback-parser-mvp/specs/production_hardening_worker.md
/Users/rene/projects/trackandback-parser-mvp/dashboard/backend/src/productionHardeningRuns.js
/Users/rene/projects/trackandback-parser-mvp/src/tb_parser_mvp/production_hardening_worker.py

data.name: Split repair-skill execution into primitive contracts before executor work
data.decision: Implement repair-skill inference as a contract-bound primitive: prompt + contract + evidence paths -> independent inference run -> structured result + proof log. Split implementation into prompt builder, schemas, artifact layout, ordered executor, and dashboard/tests.
data.rationale: This prevents the system from collapsing back into deterministic routing or recommended-skill lists. Each repair skill must become an accountable inference actor before the lifecycle can trust continuation.

data.name: Use contract-bound inference units as the system primitive
data.decision: Treat prompt + contract input + evidence paths + context -> headless inference run -> structured result + proof log as the basic primitive of the harness.
data.rationale: This keeps intelligence inside explicit inference actors while giving the deterministic lifecycle a clear enforcement role. It also prevents summaries, routing tables, recommendations, and controller logic from substituting for actual inference judgment. The regression-boundaries section records the failure modes that are no longer legitimate now that this primitive is explicit.

sections.title: Acceptance Criteria
sections.rationale: Defines objective-tight closure gates. Every criterion maps to a phrase in the objective and names the proof needed for acceptance.

data.name: Standalone lifecycle runner starts from a living doc
data.objectiveTerm: carry a living doc instance from its initial objective-bearing state toward that instance's objective completion; run Codex headless outside the user's active session
data.criterion: A standalone command or script can start a bounded headless Codex run from a living-doc JSON objective without depending on the current interactive Codex session.
data.proofRequired: Runnable standalone harness command, sample input living doc, isolated process evidence, persisted run state, and captured Codex turn/log references.
data.acceptanceTest: A reviewer can run the command against a sample living doc in a separate process and see a new run directory with contract, state, events, artifacts, native trace refs, and no dependency on chat memory.
data.currentProof: scripts/living-doc-harness-runner.mjs creates standalone run directories and can launch codex exec --json outside the active session. The live run ldh-20260507T113500Z-doc-living-doc-agentic-harness executed Codex headless with exitCode 0 and persisted wrapper logs, last message, and run state outside chat memory. The native trace summary traces/rollout-2026-05-07T17-33-58-019e01c9-6ef6-7171-a471-f07d59f9aba9.summary.json is attached to the run.

data.name: Each run has frozen contract and state metadata
data.objectiveTerm: persist each run as a frozen contract with state and event metadata
data.criterion: Each harness run writes durable contract, state, events, skill invocation, trace, and artifact metadata outside chat history.
data.proofRequired: .living-doc-runs/<run-id>/contract.json, state.json, events.jsonl, skill-invocations.jsonl, codex-turns, and artifacts with deterministic schema or contract tests.
data.acceptanceTest: Deleting chat history does not prevent a reviewer from reconstructing run objective, stage, stop, skill actions, proof status, and next action from the run directory.
data.currentProof: scripts/living-doc-harness-runner.mjs writes contract.json, state.json, events.jsonl, prompt.md, codex-turn artifact paths, and native trace refs. scripts/living-doc-harness-iteration.mjs adds iteration evidence, stop verdict, handover, terminal, proof, validation, evidence bundle, dashboard, and finalized event artifacts. tests/contract/living-doc-harness-runner.spec.mjs and tests/contract/living-doc-harness-iteration.spec.mjs cover the durable artifact spine.

data.name: Native inference logs are inspected
data.objectiveTerm: inspect the native headless inference logs as hard evidence; durable artifacts and native inference traces rather than chat memory or wrapper summaries
data.criterion: The harness locates and reads native headless inference logs for each run and records sanitized trace references and stop-evidence summaries.
data.proofRequired: Native trace discovery implementation, fixture or sample native log, sanitized trace summary, evidence bundle trace refs, and a contract test that fails when only wrapper output is available.
data.acceptanceTest: A sample run with a misleading wrapper summary is diagnosed from native inference-log evidence, and a run without native trace evidence cannot be accepted as closed.
data.currentProof: scripts/living-doc-harness-trace-reader.mjs discovers, summarizes, and attaches native Codex JSONL traces without copying private payloads. The live run has native trace hash sha256:6a88f71843c724c455ce58178a6fb5d28f996f75fc38ed4ba366049a49a121c8, 264 trace lines, source exec, cliVersion 0.128.0, and rawPayloadIncluded false.

data.name: Stop-negotiation layer diagnoses worker stops
data.objectiveTerm: treat worker stops as raw evidence, diagnose those stops through a separate reviewer-inference step
data.criterion: The worker's final message is not trusted as the stop reason; after each worker iteration, a separate reviewer inference reads the run evidence and emits the structured stop verdict that the lifecycle controller must use.
data.proofRequired: A reviewer-inference command or harness step with its own prompt/input contract, native trace reference or inference transcript, structured JSON verdict, schema validation, and lifecycle-result reference. The verdict must consume final message, native inference logs, trace refs, tool calls, edits, proof results, and living-doc before/after state.
data.acceptanceTest: A synthetic premature handoff and a real two-iteration calibration run both produce reviewer-inference verdict artifacts. The lifecycle result is invalid if it closes or continues without referencing the reviewer verdict used for that transition.
data.currentProof: Satisfied by the live two-phase lifecycle run and corrected reviewer rule. Worker stops are frozen into evidence, but reviewer inference must directly inspect the raw worker JSONL paths before its stop verdict can be accepted.

data.name: Reviewer inference emits the authoritative lifecycle signal
data.objectiveTerm: run a separate reviewer-inference step after each worker iteration that reasons over the living doc, source artifacts, acceptance criteria, proof gates, worker final message, and native inference traces to emit the authoritative structured stop signal
data.criterion: Every lifecycle transition after a worker run is backed by a reviewer-inference verdict artifact produced after direct inspection of the raw worker inference JSONL logs. The artifact must say whether the next state is closed, repairable, resumable, true-blocked, pivoted, deferred, or budget-exhausted, include a reasonCode, cite the raw JSONL path evidence it read, and state whether closureAllowed is true. Deterministic code may reject malformed or impossible verdicts, but it cannot create the authoritative meaning signal itself.
data.proofRequired: .living-doc-runs/<run-id>/reviewer-inference/iteration-N-verdict.json as the lifecycle verdict artifact, plus inference-units/iteration-N/02-reviewer-inference/prompt.md, input-contract.json, codex-events.jsonl, last-message.txt, result.json, and validation.json as the contract-bound inference proof. The reviewer input contract must carry rawWorkerJsonlPaths and requiredInspectionPaths; the reviewer codex-events log must show command_execution entries against those raw JSONL paths; the iteration proof and lifecycle result must reference the verdict used for the transition; the dashboard must display the verdict; and contract tests must prove closure fails when the reviewer verdict is missing or when reviewer inference did not inspect the raw worker JSONL path.
data.acceptanceTest: Run the two-phase calibration doc through one lifecycle command. Iteration 1 must have a reviewer verdict that keeps the loop going, iteration 2 must have a reviewer verdict that allows closure, lifecycle-result.json must reference those verdict artifacts, and the reviewer codex-events log must show command_execution entries against each raw worker JSONL path before closure is accepted.
data.currentProof: Live proof complete under the corrected raw-log rule, and local contract proof now also shows reviewer inference using the shared contract-bound inference-unit primitive. scripts/living-doc-harness-reviewer-inference.mjs delegates reviewer execution/proof to scripts/living-doc-harness-inference-unit.mjs with unitId reviewer-inference, role reviewer, sequence 02, and requiredInspectionPaths copied from rawWorkerJsonlPaths. The legacy reviewer verdict artifact remains the lifecycle reference, but it now records inferenceUnitResultPath, inferenceUnitValidationPath, inferenceUnitInputContractPath, inferenceUnitPromptPath, codexEventsPath, and stderrPath under inference-units/iteration-N/02-reviewer-inference. tests/contract/living-doc-harness-iteration.spec.mjs proves those artifacts exist, validates the unit result schema living-doc-contract-bound-inference-result/v1, checks requiredInspectionPaths, rejects missing reviewer verdicts, rejects hard-gate-invalid closure, and rejects headless reviewer runs whose codex-events do not inspect the required raw JSONL path.

data.name: Post-review inference chain is selected from contracts
data.objectiveTerm: only the initial worker and reviewer are predetermined; every later closure, repair, readiness, blocker, or next-worker unit is selected from the previous output contract and current living-doc/proof state
data.criterion: A lifecycle run starts with exactly the fixed bootstrap pair of worker inference followed by reviewer inference. After the reviewer verdict, the controller must not run a static post-review sequence. It must choose the next unit from an explicit output-input contract: close only when closure is selected and proof ordering is coherent; run balance scan only when repair is selected; run ordered repair skills only from balance-scan output; run readiness only when a repaired or resumed state asks for it; start a next worker only when the current contract authorizes continuation; and write a terminal blocker when no valid next unit exists.
data.proofRequired: Lifecycle output-input schema that names nextUnit or terminalAction, controller implementation that dispatches from that contract rather than a hard-coded post-review list, artifacts showing each selected unit received the previous output contract, dashboard graph edges showing selected handoffs, and contract tests for one-worker closure, repair-chain continuation, blocked closure-review ordering, and invalid static continuation.
data.acceptanceTest: A closable one-worker calibration run produces worker -> reviewer -> selected terminal closure path without running repair or readiness units. A repairable calibration run produces worker -> reviewer -> balance scan -> ordered repair skills -> selected next worker only because those units are named by contracts. A regression fixture fails if closure-review, balance-scan, readiness, repair, or next worker runs solely because it is prewired after reviewer output.
data.currentProof: Satisfied at contract level and post-fix standalone proof by the #215 implementation slice. finalizeHarnessIteration now builds a living-doc-harness-post-review-selection/v1 artifact from the reviewer verdict and proof state before dispatching post-review work. Closure review and repair-chain execution are dispatched from that selected nextUnit, not from an implicit always-next branch. The lifecycle output-input artifact now carries postReviewSelection, nextUnit, terminalAction, and postReviewSelectionPath. Closure review receives the finalized iteration evidence snapshot rather than the pre-final evidence input, and denied closure review is converted into a true-blocked lifecycle result with closureReviewResultPath and post-review selection evidence. tests/contract/living-doc-harness-lifecycle-controller.spec.mjs proves one-worker closure, denied closure-review result writing, nextUnit/terminalAction output-input fields, and post-review selection artifacts; npm run test:contract passes. Post-fix standalone run ldhl-20260509T100231Z-living-doc-harness-calibration-closable closed in one iteration with output-input nextUnit closure-review, terminalAction closed, postReviewSelectionPath, closureReviewResultPath, and proofValid true. Dashboard-started lifecycle ldhl-20260509T103538Z-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration extends the proof beyond the closable one-worker case: iteration 1 output-input selected nextUnit living-doc-balance-scan and allowed start-next-worker-iteration only after the repair chain completed with commitIntent evidence; iteration 2 output-input selected nextUnit closure-review and terminalAction closed. The relevant contracts are .living-doc-runs/ldh-20260509T103539Z-doc-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration/output-input/iteration-1.json and .living-doc-runs/ldh-20260509T103540Z-doc-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration/output-input/iteration-2.json.  Fresh live repair proof ldhl-20260509T111849Z-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration: .living-doc-runs/ldh-20260509T111850Z-doc-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration/output-input/iteration-1.json selected living-doc-balance-scan after reviewer repairable; the repair chain followed the live balance-scan orderedSkills output rather than a predetermined static sequence; .living-doc-runs/ldh-20260509T111851Z-doc-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration/output-input/iteration-2.json selected closure-review and terminalAction closed.

data.name: Stop verdicts route to living-doc skills
data.objectiveTerm: route repairable stops through the appropriate living-doc skills
data.criterion: The harness maps stop verdicts and stages to shaping, readiness, balance, repair, closure, blocker, or resume actions without manual skill selection.
data.proofRequired: Routing table or implementation plus tests for objective ambiguity, not executable, out of balance, premature handoff, closure candidate, proof failure, and true block.
data.acceptanceTest: Given representative stop verdict fixtures, the harness invokes or recommends the correct skill/action and records that routing in skill-invocations.jsonl.
data.currentProof: Satisfied only for routing and handover. scripts/living-doc-harness-skill-router.mjs maps stop verdicts to recommended skills and writes skill-routing, skill-invocation, and handover artifacts. This does not execute repair skills; #201 owns independent repair-skill inference execution.

data.name: Living doc is patched and rendered between attempts
data.objectiveTerm: patch and render the living doc between attempts
data.criterion: When a repair skill changes the living doc, the harness persists before/after JSON, renders the updated HTML, and resumes from the repaired state.
data.proofRequired: Before/after living-doc artifacts, rendered HTML path, render command result, and resume handover linking unresolved false terms to the next run.
data.acceptanceTest: A repairable sample run changes the living doc, produces a rendered HTML update, records the patch in events, and resumes from the updated objective state.
data.currentProof: scripts/living-doc-harness-skill-router.mjs persists before/after living-doc snapshots, unresolved objective terms, unproven criteria, skill actions, wrapper/native mismatch metadata, and render results for repaired after-docs. The live run changed and rendered docs/living-doc-agentic-harness.json/html, then iteration finalization wrote a closed terminal state, proof artifact, evidence bundle, and dashboard.

data.name: Single lifecycle command iterates until terminal state
data.objectiveTerm: carry a living doc instance from its initial objective-bearing state toward that instance's objective completion with minimal user intervention; route repairable stops; patch and render the living doc between attempts; distinguish resumed repair, valid closure, true block, pivot, deferral, and budget exhaustion
data.criterion: A single lifecycle command or backend controller owns repeated worker, reviewer-inference, evidence, repair or resume, render, and finalization iterations until the living doc reaches a valid terminal state: closed, true-blocked, pivoted, deferred, or budget-exhausted. The supervising chat must not manually start the next iteration, patch the doc, judge the stop, or decide completion.
data.proofRequired: Executable lifecycle controller, CLI or backend entry point, max-iteration/budget guard, run-state transition records, automatic repair/resume iteration start when reviewer verdict allows it, terminal-state stop behavior, dashboard visibility, and contract tests proving that a multi-iteration fixture reaches terminal state without manual supervisor edits or deterministic proxy stop judgment.

The next worker iteration cannot treat the diagram as authoritative proof unless the relationship has objective and criterion anchors.

sections.title: Regression Boundaries
sections.rationale: Makes explicit which prior failure modes are no longer legitimate interpretations of the objective. These are not reminders; they are objective-bound anti-regression contracts.

data.name: Recommendation is not execution
data.forbiddenMistake: Treating a recommended skill, routing artifact, handover, or ordered list as if the repair skill actually ran.
data.boundary: A repair skill has not run unless it has its own contract-bound inference unit with prompt, contract input, evidence paths, structured result, validation artifact, and proof log.
data.violationSignal: A lifecycle transition continues from skill-routing-iteration-N.json, skill-invocations.jsonl, or a handover without a corresponding repair-skill run directory and result artifact.
data.requiredInstead: Start the required headless inference unit for the skill and validate its result before continuing.

data.name: Routing is not repair
data.forbiddenMistake: Claiming the living doc was repaired because the router selected living-doc-balance-scan, catalytic-repair-run, or another repair skill.
data.boundary: Routing only chooses the next inference actor. Repair only exists after the actor runs, inspects evidence, changes or no-ops the doc under contract, and writes proof.
data.violationSignal: A repairable verdict produces only recommended actions and the lifecycle starts the next worker without balance-scan and ordered repair-skill run artifacts.
data.requiredInstead: Run living-doc-balance-scan first, then run every ordered repair skill as a separate headless inference run.

data.name: Summary is not required inspection
data.forbiddenMistake: Using summaries, proof gates, wrapper output, or final messages as substitutes for evidence paths that the contract requires direct inspection of.
data.boundary: Summaries may provide context, but direct evidence-path inspection is mandatory when the prompt and contract require it.
data.violationSignal: Reviewer or repair verdict basis cites only summaries while the unit log lacks command executions against required raw worker JSONL paths.
data.requiredInstead: Pass raw evidence paths into the inference unit and reject the result if its proof log does not show the required inspection.
data.proofSurface: reviewer-inference rawWorkerJsonlPaths, reviewer codex-events log, criterion-reviewer-inference-verdict.

data.name: Controller is not the meaning-maker
data.forbiddenMistake: Letting deterministic lifecycle code decide closure, repair order, repair outcome, readiness, or proof sufficiency without the required inference unit.
data.boundary: The controller chains units and enforces contracts. It does not replace worker, reviewer, balance-scan, repair, readiness, or closure-review inference judgment.
data.violationSignal: A lifecycle transition is based on deterministic sourceState, proof gates, or routing tables without a structured inference-unit result and proof log.
data.requiredInstead: Start the responsible contract-bound inference unit and use deterministic code only to validate and enforce its result.

data.name: Skill ran means own proof log
data.forbiddenMistake: Saying a skill ran when no separate headless skill inference log exists.
data.boundary: A valid skill invocation requires its own prompt, input contract, codex-events.jsonl, last message, result JSON, validation JSON, and lifecycle-visible artifact references.
data.violationSignal: skill-invocations.jsonl says a skill is recommended or completed but no ordered repair-skill run directory contains the required proof files.
data.requiredInstead: Create and validate a repair-skill run directory for each ordered skill before marking the skill complete.

data.name: Repairable does not resume worker directly
data.forbiddenMistake: Starting the next worker iteration immediately after a repairable verdict.
data.boundary: A repairable verdict must enter the repair-skill execution phase first: balance scan, ordered skill chain, validation, render proof, then readiness or review.
data.violationSignal: Lifecycle output-input starts a worker after repairable while repair-skill run artifacts are absent or incomplete.
data.requiredInstead: Run the full ordered repair-skill chain or stop with a blocker before worker resume.

data.name: Buffered log is not live observation
data.invariant: A supervisor or dashboard is not directly observing inference if codex-events.jsonl is only written after the child inference exits.
data.failureMode: The system can claim it inspected inference while only doing retrospective artifact review.
data.requiredBehavior: Headless inference stdout and stderr must stream to durable files during the run, and dashboard/supervisor APIs must be able to tail repair-unit codex-events before result.json exists.
evidence: scripts/living-doc-harness-inference-unit.mjs streams codex-events.jsonl and stderr.log while child inference is running.
tests/contract/living-doc-harness-inference-unit.spec.mjs asserts codex-events.jsonl contains required path evidence before the fake child exits.
scripts/living-doc-harness-dashboard-server.mjs exposes /api/runs/:runId/repair-units and /api/runs/:runId/repair-units/:iteration/:unit/tail for direct local repair-unit log inspection.
tests/contract/living-doc-harness-dashboard-server.spec.mjs proves the dashboard tail endpoint sees command_execution evidence before the repair unit has result.json.

sections.title: Relationship Maps
sections.rationale: Makes relationship structure a first-class semantic surface. Mermaid is used as portable notation, but each map is defended by a relationship claim, anchors, validation question, and drift risk.

data.name: Contract-bound inference unit map
data.claim: Every intelligent actor in the harness is the same primitive: prompt plus contract input plus evidence paths plus context enters a headless inference run, and a structured result plus proof log exits into lifecycle enforcement.
data.semanticRole: Normative: defines the primitive that implementation and tests must preserve across worker, reviewer, balance scan, repair skill, readiness, and closure review actors.
data.validationQuestion: Can every lifecycle transition point to a concrete inference unit with promptPath, inputContractPath, evidencePaths, runDir, codexEventsPath, resultPath, validationPath, and lifecycleEffect?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if deterministic code makes closure, repair, readiness, or proof-sufficiency decisions without an inference-unit result and proof log.

diagrams.title: Primitive Flow
diagrams.text:
flowchart LR
  Prompt["Role prompt"] --> Unit["Headless inference unit"]
  Contract["Input contract"] --> Unit
  Evidence["Evidence paths"] --> Unit
  Context["Summary context"] --> Unit
  Unit --> Result["Structured result"]
  Unit --> Log["Own proof log"]
  Result --> Validator["Deterministic schema and gate validation"]
  Log --> Validator
  Validator --> Lifecycle["Allowed lifecycle effect"]

data.name: Lifecycle output-input chain map
data.claim: The lifecycle is a chain of contract-bound inference units where each unit output becomes validated input for the next unit until closure, repair, true block, pivot, deferral, budget exhaustion, or resume is justified.
data.semanticRole: Execution-guiding: prevents the lifecycle from collapsing worker, reviewer, repair, readiness, and closure into one undifferentiated loop.
data.validationQuestion: After a worker stop, does the system create the reviewer unit, inspect raw worker JSONL, validate the verdict, and route to the next unit instead of deciding from wrapper output or chat context?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if the lifecycle gains a shortcut that resumes worker execution, closes the run, or marks repair complete without the required downstream inference unit.

diagrams.title: Lifecycle Chain
diagrams.text:
flowchart TD
  Start["Initial objective-bearing living doc"] --> Worker["Worker inference unit"]
  Worker --> WorkerLog["Raw worker JSONL"]
  WorkerLog --> Reviewer["Reviewer inference unit"]
  Reviewer --> Verdict{"Structured verdict"}
  Verdict -->|closed| ClosureReview["Closure review inference"]
  Verdict -->|repairable| BalanceScan["Balance-scan inference"]
  Verdict -->|unclear| Readiness["Readiness inference"]
  Verdict -->|blocked| Blocker["True blocker record"]
  BalanceScan --> RepairChain["Ordered repair-skill units"]
  RepairChain --> Render["Render updated living doc"]
  Render --> Readiness
  Readiness --> Worker
  ClosureReview --> Terminal["Valid terminal state"]

data.name: Ordered repair-skill chain map
data.claim: A repairable verdict enters a repair phase: balance scan creates an ordered skill list, and every selected skill runs as its own inference unit before the worker may resume.
data.semanticRole: Diagnostic and execution-guiding: separates recommendation, routing, independent skill execution, doc-changing proof, no-op proof, and blocker creation.
data.validationQuestion: If balance scan returns five skills, can the evidence bundle show five ordered skill run directories with prompts, input contracts, raw JSONL logs, structured results, validation files, and handoff paths?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if skill-invocations.jsonl or a recommended list is accepted as proof that repair happened.

diagrams.title: Repair Skill Chain
diagrams.text:
flowchart LR
  Repairable["Reviewer verdict: repairable"] --> Scan["living-doc-balance-scan unit"]
  Scan --> Order["Ordered repair list"]
  Order --> Skill1["Repair skill 1 unit"]
  Skill1 --> Proof1["Result plus log plus validation"]
  Proof1 --> Skill2["Repair skill 2 unit"]
  Skill2 --> Proof2["Result plus log plus validation"]
  Proof2 --> SkillN["Repair skill N unit"]
  SkillN --> Render["Render changed living doc"]
  Render --> Ready["Readiness or closure-review unit"]
  Skill1 -->|blocked| Block["Blocker record"]
  Skill2 -->|blocked| Block
  SkillN -->|blocked| Block

data.name: Dashboard inference-unit graph map
data.claim: The dashboard graph is the visual form of the lifecycle contract chain: nodes are inference units or terminal artifacts, and arrows are contract handoffs backed by concrete artifact paths.
data.semanticRole: Operational dashboard contract: prevents the graph from becoming a static explanation page by requiring every visible card and arrow to come from lifecycle/run artifacts.
data.validationQuestion: For every graph node and edge, can the dashboard show the artifact that created it and the contract evidence it depends on?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if the dashboard renders hard-coded mock nodes, omits raw JSONL evidence-path references, hides running/prepared repair units, or cannot explain why a lifecycle closed, continued, repaired, or blocked.

diagrams.title: Artifact-Derived Dashboard Graph
diagrams.text:
flowchart LR
  Lifecycle["lifecycle-result.json"] --> Mapper["artifact-to-graph mapper"]
  Run["run contract and state"] --> Mapper
  WorkerLog["worker raw JSONL paths"] --> Mapper
  Reviewer["reviewer verdict"] --> Mapper
  Repair["repair unit directories"] --> Mapper
  Terminal["terminal and blocker artifacts"] --> Mapper
  Mapper --> GraphAPI["/api/lifecycles/:id/graph"]
  GraphAPI --> Dashboard["dashboard graph view"]
  Dashboard --> Inspector["contract edge inspector"]
  Dashboard --> Tail["local raw log tail when allowed"]
  Issue["GitHub issue refs"] --> Mapper

data.name: Diagram semantics boundary map
data.claim: A Mermaid diagram in a living doc is not proof, decoration, or a new source of truth; it is a portable projection of anchored relationships that helps inference inspect the doc structure.
data.semanticRole: Governance: defends why relationship-map exists as a convergence type and prevents Mermaid from becoming an ungrounded appendix.
data.validationQuestion: Can every map name the relationship it asserts, the anchors it depends on, the decision it supports, and the condition that would make it stale?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if diagrams are added without anchors, validation questions, or drift risks, or if a diagram is treated as proof that the system works.

diagrams.title: Semantic Defense
diagrams.text:
flowchart TD
  RelationshipClaim["Relationship claim"] --> Map["Relationship map card"]
  Anchors["Sections, criteria, invariants, issues, code"] --> Map
  Mermaid["Mermaid source"] --> Map
  Map --> Inspection["Inspectable structure"]
  Inspection --> Question["Validation question"]
  Question --> Decision["Reasoning, repair, or proof decision"]
  Map --> DriftRisk["Staleness trigger"]
  DriftRisk --> Repair["Update or remove map"]

data.name: Diagram objective criteria alignment map
data.claim: A relationship-map diagram is safe to use for reasoning only when its claim is tied to objective terms and acceptance criteria that can test the relationship it shows.
data.semanticRole: Diagnostic and repair-guiding: defines when relationship-map-alignment must run and what it compares before diagrams influence execution, repair, or closure reasoning.
data.validationQuestion: For every diagram, can the system name the objective term it serves, the acceptance criterion that tests it, the anchors that ground it, and the drift signal that would make it invalid?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if diagrams are edited without updating criterion anchors, if criteria change without updating the map, or if Mermaid implies a proof/lifecycle path not required by the objective.

diagrams.title: Diagram Alignment Repair Loop
diagrams.text:
flowchart TD
  Objective["Objective and success condition"] --> Ledger["Objective term ledger"]
  Criteria["Acceptance criteria"] --> Ledger
  Diagram["Relationship-map claim and Mermaid"] --> DiagramLedger["Diagram relationship ledger"]
  Anchors["Sections, criteria, invariants, issues, code"] --> DiagramLedger
  Ledger --> Compare["Alignment comparison"]
  DiagramLedger --> Compare
  Compare -->|aligned| Continue["Use map for reasoning"]
  Compare -->|missing criterion| CriteriaGap["Criteria gap"]
  Compare -->|beyond objective| ObjectiveGap["Objective gap"]
  Compare -->|overclaim or stale| Repair["Repair map, anchors, or drift risk"]
  Repair --> Render["Render living doc"]
  CriteriaGap --> Shape["Objective acceptance shaping"]
  ObjectiveGap --> Block["Block, follow-up, or out-of-scope"]

data.name: Owned lifecycle controller
data.summary: scripts/living-doc-harness-lifecycle.mjs now owns the output-input channel between iterations. It consumes each finalized iteration, writes a durable output-input artifact, passes lifecycle input into the next worker prompt, starts the next iteration automatically when repair/resume is allowed, and stops on valid terminal state or budget exhaustion.

The lifecycle controller must stop only on closed, true-blocked, pivoted, deferred, budget-exhausted, or an explicit process defect that has been ticketed.

data.name: Repair skill inference runner
data.summary: Implemented and live-proven lifecycle-owned executor that turns repairable reviewer verdicts into a sequential contract-bound inference chain: living-doc-balance-scan first, then every skill in the ordered list returned by the scan. The chain writes independent unit artifacts, prior-result handoff, skill invocation entries, repair-chain result, output-input repairSkillResultPath, evidence bundle visibility, and dashboard visibility. Live calibration run ldh-20260508T073001Z-doc-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration executed six ordered repair units and verified required evidence-path inspection through raw codex-events logs. The #208 policy fix makes doc-changing repair units commit-intent-only: units may propose detailed commit metadata, but must not mutate the git index. Dashboard-started lifecycle ldhl-20260509T103538Z-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration proves the chain no longer blocks on implicit git commit and preserves commitIntent in the repair-chain result.  Fresh live run ldhl-20260509T111849Z-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration proves the current implementation after #208: catalytic-repair-run changed the calibration living doc and emitted commitIntent, objective-execution-readiness completed as a second ordered live inference unit, and the lifecycle closed on the next iteration.
responsibilities: Read the reviewer verdict and repair handover for a repairable stop.

Validate each skill result before starting the next skill.

Apply commit-intent-only policy to every repair-skill unit: forbid direct git commits, preserve detailed commitIntent, and surface commit-policy failures with a distinct reason code.

sections.rationale: Keeps a short plain-language statement of what the living doc is trying to make true.

data.name: Plain state
data.objectiveExplanation: This doc is about building a standalone governed loop that can take one living doc from its starting objective state toward that doc's real completion while recording every stop, repair, proof claim, and blocker outside chat memory.
data.currentStateExplanation: The harness can run standalone lifecycle loops, require reviewer verdicts, execute ordered repair skills as independent inference units, expose live repair-unit logs, and now render the lifecycle as an artifact-derived inference-unit graph in the local dashboard.
data.text: Epic #209 is now partly real through both backend data and UI. The dashboard can list lifecycle results, fetch an artifact-derived graph, render worker/reviewer/repair/terminal/blocker/issue cards with contract arrows, inspect edge evidence paths, and tail repair-unit logs from graph nodes. The remaining dashboard gap is broader calibration against real long-running lifecycle data and polish where those artifacts expose layout or interpretation issues.
